ddls:
CREATE TABLE "education_levels" (
  "education_level_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each education level */,
  "education_level" TEXT /* Education level (e.g., HS-grad, Some-college) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the education level */,
  "is_higher_education" INTEGER /* Indicates if the education level is considered higher education */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("education_level_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "workclasses" (
  "workclass_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each workclass */,
  "workclass" TEXT /* Workclass (e.g., Private, Government) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the workclass */,
  "is_government" INTEGER /* Indicates if the workclass is in the government sector */,
  "is_private" INTEGER /* Indicates if the workclass is in the private sector */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("workclass_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "marital_statuses" (
  "marital_status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each marital status */,
  "marital_status" TEXT /* Marital status (e.g., Married-civ-spouse, Never-married) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the marital status */,
  "is_married" INTEGER /* Indicates if the marital status is married */,
  "is_never_married" INTEGER /* Indicates if the marital status is never married */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("marital_status_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "occupations" (
  "occupation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each occupation */,
  "occupation" TEXT /* Occupation (e.g., Exec-managerial, Sales) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the occupation */,
  "is_management" INTEGER /* Indicates if the occupation is in management */,
  "is_professional" INTEGER /* Indicates if the occupation is professional */,
  "is_service" INTEGER /* Indicates if the occupation is in service */,
  "is_sales" INTEGER /* Indicates if the occupation is in sales */,
  "is_technical" INTEGER /* Indicates if the occupation is technical */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("occupation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "relationships" (
  "relationship_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each relationship */,
  "relationship" TEXT /* Relationship (e.g., Husband, Own-child) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the relationship */,
  "is_spouse" INTEGER /* Indicates if the relationship is a spouse */,
  "is_child" INTEGER /* Indicates if the relationship is a child */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("relationship_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "races" (
  "race_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each race */,
  "race" TEXT /* Race (e.g., White, Black) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the race */,
  "is_minority" INTEGER /* Indicates if the race is considered a minority */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("race_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "incomes" (
  "income_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each income */,
  "income" TEXT /* Income (e.g., <=50K, >50K) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the income */,
  "is_high_income" INTEGER /* Indicates if the income is considered high */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("income_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, analyst, admin) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for security */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user account is active */,
  "is_admin" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user has administrative privileges */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
column_names: Unique identifier for each education level
